name: Ensure KMSKeys are not exposed through publicly accessible VMs
description: KMSKeys that are exposed by publicly accessible VMs.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation strategy
1. Remove public management exposure from VMs.
2. Limit KMS key access to approved identities only.

## AWS
~~~bash
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress --group-id <sg-id> --protocol tcp --port 22 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress --group-id <sg-id> --protocol tcp --port 3389 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0
~~~

## GCP
~~~bash
gcloud compute firewall-rules update <rule-name> --source-ranges=<trusted-cidr>
gcloud kms keys remove-iam-policy-binding <key-name> --location <location> --keyring <keyring> --member="allUsers" --role="roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyEncrypterDecrypter"
~~~

## Azure
~~~bash
az network nsg rule update --resource-group <rg> --nsg-name <nsg> --name <rule> --source-address-prefixes <trusted-cidr>
az role assignment delete --assignee <principal-id> --role "Key Vault Crypto User" --scope <key-vault-or-key-scope>
~~~

### References
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/key-policies.html
- https://cloud.google.com/kms/docs/reference/permissions-and-roles
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/general/rbac-guide
body:
### Security intent

This control protects cryptographic boundaries by ensuring that **kMSKeys are not exposed through publicly accessible VMs**.

### Key state reviewed

The assessment reviews kMSKeys that are exposed by publicly accessible VMs. in AWS, Google Cloud, Alibaba Cloud, and Azure / Microsoft Entra. It focuses on key access, lifecycle, rotation, deletion, exposure, or encryption usage depending on the control.

### Specialist guidance

Encryption controls should be remediated with dependency awareness. Before disabling, rotating, deleting, or replacing a key, confirm which services use it, validate decrypt paths, and keep old key material available until dependent workloads are safely migrated.
